name: v05-data-export
description: Paginated data export pipeline with per-page resilience. Receives a page list, fetches each page sequentially (rate-limit friendly), and falls back to a DLQ entry when a page fetch fails after retries. Each page fetch carries `retry: { maxAttempts: 3 }` and a `maxDuration: 5s` cap so transient failures self-heal but pathological hangs don't stall the whole export. Successful pages contribute to a final assemble step that summarizes total rows + DLQ count. Demonstrates: forEach + tryCatch nested with per-step retry + maxDuration, resilient ETL pattern that surfaces failed pages without aborting the run.

inputs.expression: ({ page: ctx.state.page.number, ok: ctx.state['fetch-page'] !== undefined, rowCount: ctx.state['fetch-page'] && ctx.state['fetch-page'].args ? Object.keys(ctx.state['fetch-page'].args).length : 0, error: ctx.state.pageError || null })

((arr) => { const summaries = arr.map(r => (r && r.data && typeof r.data === 'object') ? r.data : r); const ok = summaries.filter(s => s && s.ok === true); const failed = summaries.filter(s => s && s.ok === false); return { exported: true, totalPages: summaries.length, successfulPages: ok.length, totalRows: ok.reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.rowCount || 0), 0), dlqPages: failed.map(f => ({ page: f.page, error: f.error })) }; })(ctx.state['fetch-pages'] || [])
